because.text: The formal capstone. SizeBound (6b715124) proved size<=spent+1 for an ABSTRACT seven-row accounting model; bridge_check.py samples the per-step premise on live oracle traces WITHOUT row-by-row correspondence (a caveat I wrote into proofs/README twice, and flagged a future 'step-tag classifier' to close). Now that EvalMachine (633ac549) is a faithful total Lean evaluator, the bound is a theorem ABOUT IT: size_step gives the exact per-step accounting (size t'+1 <= size t+c: every action grows the term by at most cost-1) - and R-S, the only growing rule, holds UNCONDITIONALLY because the discarded <S> head is pure size slack, no leaf assumption; eval_size_bound + evalHash_size_bound carry it end-to-end on the same evalHash the differential bridge pins to the oracle byte-exact on 33 vectors. So the row correspondence is a PROOF, not a classifier. The four Lean fronts are now explicitly layered (EvalMachine -> MachineBytes -> Sha256), each standing on the proven one below. Proved directly by the maintainer via fun_induction+grind mirroring step_bounds; zero sorry; cold-ran the bridge + all four front regressions on a fresh master checkout (0999998) on the mini before filing. Only a Rust production impl remains of the Qwen formal-verification asks.

subject.note: Executable law: the ADR-001 memory bound (size<=spent+1) proven directly on the concrete Lean evaluator - the step-tag/row-correspondence gap closed by theorem
